channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Irish OL Anthonie Knapp won't play in CFP championship game; Buckeyes CB Denzel Burke good to go
item.description: Notre Dame's Anthonie Knapp has been ruled out of the College Football Playoff championship game against Ohio State with an ankle injury, but coach Marcus Freeman said Sunday Rocco Spindler is on track to play.
item.dc:creator: ERIC OLSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ex-WWE star Melina reported 'safe' after concerns over whereabouts amid Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: Pro wrestling figures raised concerns on the whereabouts of former WWE star Melina as the wildfires in Los Angeles left a path of destruction in the city.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fired US Center for SafeSport investigator arrested again, this time charged with rape
item.description: DENVER (AP) — An ex-cop fired from his job as an investigator at the U.S. Center for SafeSport for allegedly stealing money seized at a drug bust has been arrested again, this time charged with rape and sex trafficking.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: New England Patriots hire former Super Bowl champ Mike Vrabel as next coach; Bears had interviewed him
item.description: Vrabel, a fan favorite during eight seasons as a player in New England where he was a member of its first three Super Bowl winners and an inductee into the team’s Hall of Fame in 2023, was the preferred candidate because of his long association with the franchise and coaching success.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Friedrich pulls away for 4-man bobsled win, US finishes 3rd in team luge relay
item.description: ST. MORITZ, Switzerland (AP) — Francesco Friedrich got the 85th World Cup bobsled victory of his career Sunday, dominating the second heat to win a four-man race.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Tottenham avoids massive FA Cup shock with extra-time win as Man United beats Arsenal on penalties
item.description: Tottenham has endured the humiliation of being taken to extra time by Tamworth, an opponent nearly 100 places lower in English soccer’s pyramid, before winning 3-0 and avoiding one of the biggest shocks in FA Cup history.
item.dc:creator: Steve Douglas, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Lauren Macuga wins a World Cup super-G race with Lindsey Vonn 4th on stellar day for US ski team
item.description: ST. ANTON, Austria (AP) — Breakout star Lauren Macuga and comeback queen Lindsey Vonn made it a classic Sunday for the United States ski team.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Australian Open: Olympic champ Zheng Qinwen starts her bid for another final on a rainy Day 1
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Zheng Qinwen's tennis resume is quite different from what it was when she showed up at the Australian Open a full year ago.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Utah HC loses on late goal to Islanders, 2-1
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) - Mathew Barzal scored with 1:25 left in regulation and the New York Islanders defeated the Utah Hockey Club 2-1 at Delta Center on Saturday. The forward found a rebound off a scrum in front of the net and lifted it over a sprawling Connor Ingram from the right circle.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Ezra Ausar scores 21 as Utah earns first Big 12 victory
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Ezra Ausar scored a season-high 21 points in just 17 minutes of action. as the Utah basketball team won its first Big 12 Conference game Saturday, beating Oklahoma State, 83-62.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: J.J. Spaun takes the lead at Sony Open with a wide-open chase in the wind of Waialae
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Stephan Jaeger had nine birdies for the lowest round this week at Waialae, and two of the most important holes for him Saturday in the Sony Open were pars.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Auburn's Broome suffers 'significant' ankle sprain in comeback win over South Carolina
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Johni Broome suffered a “significant sprain” of his left ankle in No. 2 Auburn's win over South Carolina on Saturday, and Tigers coach Bruce Pearl said his team's leading scorer will have an MRI to determine the extent of the injury.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-NFL player Robert Quinn arrested after attempted hit-and-run in South Carolina
item.description: Former NFL player Robert Quinn was arrested in South Carolina after allegedly attempting to leave the scene of a crash, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bill McCartney, legendary Colorado coach, dead at 84 after dementia battle
item.description: Former Colorado football coach Bill McCartney, who led the team to its only national title in 1990, died Friday at 84 after a battle with dementia, his family confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CMU Women's wrestler Marissa Gallegos reflects on wrestling journey
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)—Marissa Gallegos is no stranger to the spotlight. The Colorado Mesa University star has cemented her legacy as the winningest women’s wrestler in program history. Her career, defined by grit, dominance, and determination, has inspired athletes across the sport.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Sensabaugh stays hot as Jazz beat Orlando, 105-92
item.description: ORLANDO (ABC4 Sports) - So far, 2025 has treated the Utah Jazz pretty well, especially Brice Sensabaugh.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Buccaneers beat Saints 27-19 to clinch NFC South and wide receiver Mike Evans surpasses 1,000 yards
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — Just 36 seconds away from clinching the NFC South title, the Tampa Bay Buccaneers had another goal to achieve. Take a knee on their 32-yard line and run out the clock? No chance. Mike Evans still needed 5 yards to make history.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Patriots fire coach Jerod Mayo shortly after beating Bills to finish his lone season at 4-13
item.description: FOXBOROUGH, Mass. (AP) — The New England Patriots fired coach Jerod Mayo on Sunday after one season, beginning another reboot for the franchise just a year after it parted with longtime coach Bill Belichick.
item.dc:creator: KYLE HIGHTOWER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Mariota leads playoff-bound Commanders to 23-19 win over Cowboys after replacing Daniels
item.description: Marcus Mariota threw a 5-yard touchdown pass to Terry McLaurin with 3 seconds left, lifting Washington to a 23-19 victory over the Dallas Cowboys on Sunday and clinching the sixth seed in the NFC playoffs for the Commanders.
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, Fox 5
